titlestmt.titleproper: Dutch and French engravings (portraits and allegories)8451
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-04-23 18:41:49 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Dutch and French engravings (portraits and allegories)
did.abstract: Engravings by various artists, depicting prominent individuals, including many clergymen and intellectuals, of western Europe in the 16th-18th centuries. Also, allegorical scenes, Biblical scenes, and decorative vignettes.
did.langmaterial: Collection material in English,French, and Latin

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Engravings by various artists, depicting prominent individuals, including many clergymen and intellectuals, of western Europe in the 16th-18th centuries. Many of the portraits appear to have been done in memory of their subjects shortly after their death. Included are portraits of Thomas Cromwell, Gabriel Naudé, Nicolas Caussin; John Tillotson, Archbishop of Canterbury; Jean de La Fontaine; Alexandre de Prouville, marquis de Tracy; Giovanni Battista Montano, Thierry Bignon, Edouard Colbert de Villacerf, Pierre-Daniel Huet, and Pierre Gassendi. Also included are allegorical scenes, Biblical scenes, and decorative vignettes. One engraving, "La reponce embarassante," 1700s, is hand-colored. Engravers include Nicolas de Largillierre, Claude Mellan, Robert Nanteuil, Abraham Bosse, Jacobus Houbraken, and Gerard de Jode.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Dutch and French Engravings (Portraits and Allegories), #8451. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.persname: Cromwell, Thomas, Earl of Essex, 1485?-1540 -- : Portraits.
Tracy, Alexandre de Prouville, marquis de, 1596 or 1603-1670 -- : Portraits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Engraver: Engraver unknown but most likely made in France.Information from Captions: Lived 1638-1663. Did work in Gaul (modern day France/Belgium).Additional Information: Original description in Latin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Engraver: Cl. Mellan G. del. et sc. Cartel.Information from Captions: Born in Paris, February 2, 1600. Died in Abbeville, July 29, 1653.Additional Information: Original description in French.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Engraver: Designed by Cochin le Fils, Engraved by L. CarsInformation from Captions: Designer of the king's cabinet. Additional Information: Original description In French. Lived 1699-1771.

did.unittitle: P. Nicolas Caussin of the Company of Jesus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional Information: Original description in French. Lived 1583-1951.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Information from Captions: Capuchin preacher, Died December 18, 1638.Additional Information: Original description in French. Lived 1577-1638.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Information from Captions: First Presidential Garde of Seals. Born 1584, Died January 3, 1656, at the age of 72.Additional Information: Original description in French.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Engraver: Claude MellanAdditional Information: Original description in Latin. Original from copperplate engraving.

scopecontent.p: Information from Captions: Died August (3?), 1630Additional Information: Original description in Latin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Engraver: Robert NanteuilInformation from Captions: F. Cabouret commissioned the engraving after Fronteau's death.Additional Information: Original description in Latin. French cleric. Years: 1614-1662

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Information from Captions: His fatherland/patria is listed as Sardamum. He is a disciple of Golzi, an imitator of Lysippus (an ancient sculptor).Additional Information: Original description in Latin.

did.unittitle: Mme D'Escorailles de Roussille, Duchesse de Fontanges

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional Information: Original description in French. The proper name may be Marie Angélique, the lover of Louis the Sun King.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Engraver: Philippe GalleAdditional Information: Original description in Latin. The personification of Time, winged and seated, surrounded with various attributes, riding atop a chariot carried by two deer, destroying everything on his way; accompanied by personifications of the four seasons on foot (they are labeled: Autumnus, Hijems, Aestas, Ver); ruins of classical buildings are seen in the background. -Adapted from British Museum.

did.unittitle: The Martyrdom of Christ and the Apostles (Series title)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Engraver: Hendrick GoltziusAdditional Information: Original description in Latin. Matthias is beheaded with an ax and stoned the left of the scene of his martyrdom, he is shown again, with an ax in one hand and a book in the other. Among the show twice latin two lines. This print is part of a series of 13 prints of Christ (unnumbered) and the apostles (numbered), more specifically the torture they suffered.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Engraver: Philippe GalleAdditional Information: Original description in Latin.

did.unittitle: Appearances of Christ after his death (Series title)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Engraver: Julius GoltziusAdditional Information: Original description in Latin. Appearances of Christ after his death (series title). Christ, dressed as a pilgrim, accompanies two apostles on the road to Emmaus. In the background a castle. The print has a signature Latin and is part of a series of prints on the subject of the appearances of Christ after his death.
